common.supabaseViewOnly: This screen needs your workspace to be connected. Use the main technology list from the header.
common.langPickerFooter: Spanish and English available; more languages coming soon.

fab.calendarIntro: Quick dated notes with tags (stored on this device).
fab.curiositiesIntro: Interesting tools and ideas to explore (living list).
fab.cvTipsIntro: Practical tips when you apply with SkillAtlas. Our ATS check is a lightweight heuristic; commercial tools may score differently.
fab.cvTipsBullet1: Keep multiple CV versions (up to 5 in SkillAtlas) tuned to role type or stack.
fab.cvTipsBullet2: For a one-page target: emphasize recent impact, tighten the summary, and hide secondary sections via visibility.
fab.cvTipsBullet3: Use a clear email and dated experience; bullets with metrics help both parsers and humans.
fab.cvTipsBullet4: Export to PDF and sanity-check reading order; flashy multi-column layouts can confuse some ATS.
fab.cvTipsBullet5: Short cover letters per channel (LinkedIn, email, agency) still make a strong first impression.
fab.aiIntro: We're designing AI help that fits your skills map—without replacing your judgment.
fab.aiBullet1: Suggestions when you write projects and evidence.
fab.aiBullet2: Summaries and questions about sources in Study (when RAG is available).
fab.aiBullet3: Ideas for concepts and templates—always for you to review.
fab.aiFooter: No model is connected yet; we'll notify you when the beta opens.

footer.tagline: A living map of technologies, concepts, and projects.

mobileLanding.title: Built for larger screens
mobileLanding.body: The dashboard, CV, and portfolio work best with a keyboard and mouse. On your phone you can request access or sign in.
mobileLanding.footer: Open SkillAtlas on a desktop for the full skills map, projects, and portfolio.

ats.disclaimer: This is a heuristic check based on your content and the template selected in this preview. Real ATS parsers vary; use it as a checklist, not a guarantee.

ok.experienceBullets: Some roles include bullet points (better for parsers).
ok.coverLetters: At least one cover letter has body text.

warn.noEmail: No contact email on the CV; many ATS expect one.
warn.shortSummary: Summary is very short; add more context and wins.
warn.expMissingBullets: Some roles have no bullets; add measurable outcomes.
warn.expMissingDates: Some roles are missing start/end dates.
warn.sidebarLayout: The “Sidebar” template uses columns; reading order in PDF export may confuse some ATS. Try “Classic” or “Modern” if needed.
warn.sectionExperienceHidden: The Experience section is hidden in the document.
warn.sectionSummaryHidden: Summary / bio is hidden; many recruiters skim it first.

info.noEducation: No education listed (may be fine for your profile).
info.noPhone: No phone on the CV (optional for many ATS).
info.keywordsMuted: Help stack off and no highlights block: fewer obvious keywords for simple parsers.
info.decorativeTemplate: More decorative template: double-check contrast and PDF if applying through very conservative pipelines.

cv.importPasteHint: Separate each job or degree with a blank line. First line: role at company or degree at school (use “ at ”, “ @ ”, “ | ”, or “ — ”). Optional: a date line (years or month+year). Use bullets (- or •) for the rest.
cv.importPlaceholder:
Experience example:
Data Analyst at Acme Inc.
2025 – 2026
- KPIs and reporting

Education example:
BSc Mathematics at Sample University
2016 – 2022
cv.importModalIntro: Paste a résumé or extract a PDF, then import into the form (experience, education, and any empty fields we can infer).
cv.importModalIntroManual: Map the résumé with highlights: select text in the colored view and assign each part to the form. Paste (Ctrl+V) or choose a PDF.
cv.importSurfaceEmptyHint: Paste your CV with Ctrl+V or choose a PDF.
cv.importManualHint: When you open this tab we auto-detect links, email, and sections like automatic import. Select text to get a popover with quick targets, or use “Auto-detect” to merge more suggestions.
cv.importManualSidebarEmpty: Nothing mapped yet. Assign fragments from the highlighted text.
cv.importManualSidebarExpBlockHint: Tags exp:exist / exp:new mean “fill the row already in your CV” vs “add a new row when applying”; one value per field (switching exist↔new replaces the other).
cv.importManualSidebarEduBlockHint: Tags edu:exist / edu:new mean “fill the row already in your CV” vs “add a new row when applying”; one value per field (switching exist↔new replaces the other).
cv.importManualLegendExpHint: Experience: role · company · location · start · end · bullets (rings: form row vs new row).
cv.importManualPopoverFollowDragLabel: Show assign panel while dragging (clicks pass through to the text; by default the panel only appears when you release).
cv.importManualPopoverHint: Edit the text if needed; search below and tap a row to assign (replaces any previous mapping to the same single-line field).
cv.importManualEmptyBodyToast: The text to assign cannot be empty.
cv.importManualNoSelectToast: Select text in the highlighted view before assigning.
cv.importManualNoAssignmentsToast: Assign at least one fragment to a field.
cv.importManualPickTargetToast: Pick a target in the list.
cv.importManualTextChangedToast: The text changed; manual assignments were cleared.
cv.importManualPartialErrorsToast: Some assignments had an invalid index and were skipped.
cv.importManualSuggestAutoToast: Auto-detect applied; review highlights and adjust.
cv.importManualSuggestMergedToast: Suggestions merged without overwriting your mappings.
cv.importConfirmBody: Text will be merged with your current CV: entries that already match (same role and company, degree and school, etc.) will not be duplicated. Download a JSON backup if you may want to undo.
cv.importAllDuplicatesToast: Nothing new to add: those entries were already in the form.
cv.importDedupeSuffix: ({{n}} entries already existed and were not duplicated).
cv.importReplaceSummaryConfirm: Replace your CV summary with the text detected from the imported document?
cv.importAssistHint: Insert section headings at the cursor, or move the selection to the end under the right heading.
cv.importMovedBlockToast: Text moved to the end under heading {{h}}. Review and remove duplicates if needed.
cv.importToolsHint: Open the modal to paste text or a PDF and merge into the form.
cv.backupHint: Download JSON before clearing or testing imports. Project selection on the CV is not cleared.
cv.clearContentConfirm: Clear experience, education, certifications, languages, and CV text fields? (Does not remove your name in Settings or project selection.)
cv.clearModalBody: This removes experience, education, certifications, languages, and CV text fields. It does not change your name in Settings or your project selection for the CV.
cv.projectsSectionHintCompact: Optional: your portfolio already showcases work. Pick a few projects for the PDF; one can be featured, the rest stay compact.
cv.featuredOnlyDoneToast: Only the featured project stays on the CV.
cv.backupRestoreInvalid: This file is not a valid backup.
cv.importBothToast: Added {{exp}} experience block(s) and {{edu}} education block(s).
cv.importPdfPasteHint: After a PDF import, text lands here; you may need to tidy it, then use “Import experience and education” (or the section-only buttons).
cv.importParseFailedToast: No valid blocks found; check the format.
cv.importParseFailedHints: Leave a blank line between roles or degrees; add headings like “Experience” / “Education” when you can. If the PDF is a scan, copy text from the viewer or try “Experience only” / “Education only”.
cv.importReviewNote: Always review imported data. We plan to integrate AI later to help with imports from PDF and other formats.
cv.publicIntro: Shared link. If it doesn’t exist or is disabled, you’ll see a warning.
cv.publicNoSupabase: This page needs a connected workspace.
cv.publicLoadError: Could not load the public CV.
cv.publicNotFound: This CV does not exist or is not published.
cv.docLimitToast: You can store up to 5 résumés.
cv.docNameDialogHint: Pick a name so you can tell them apart in the selector.
cv.docDeleteOnly: You can’t delete your only résumé.
cv.docDeleteConfirm: Delete this résumé? This can’t be undone.
cv.docManageMainLabel: Primary résumé (public link uses this document)
cv.intro: Edit your résumé here (details, experience, education, certifications, languages), pick projects and a template, import from pasted text or PDF, open preview with section order, and print or save as PDF. Enable the public token link under Settings → Public portfolio.
cv.importPdfToast: Text extracted from the PDF. Review the import modal and tap “Import into form”.
cv.importPdfEmptyText: No readable text was extracted from the PDF.
cv.publicShareMovedNote: The public CV link is managed in Settings → “Public portfolio”.
cv.printHint: Use “Save as PDF” in your browser’s print dialog.
cv.publicShareHint: Generate a revocable link to share your CV.
cv.publicShareSettingsHint: This link is managed in Settings and can be revoked (you can regenerate it).
cv.publicShareWarn: When you regenerate, the previous link stops working.
cv.publicShareNoUrl: Enable the link to copy it.
cv.needSession: Sign in to view your CV.
cv.highlightsHint: One line per bullet. It will be printed as a list.
cv.settingsSyncHint: Name, bio, avatar and stack sync with Settings. If they are empty, you can fill them here.
cv.sectionVisibilityHint: Uncheck sections you don’t want in preview or print.
cv.bubbleTipsTitle: CV tips (same panel as the floating bubble)
cv.settingsModalSubtitle: Section visibility, PDF target, and date formatting.
cv.coverTabIntro: Cover letters live outside the main CV body; turn them on in document visibility if you want them in the exported PDF.
cv.jobOffersIntro: Paste links, drag cards between columns, and add notes. Data is saved in your preferences (this device / account).
cv.formatPrefsHint: Dates, locations, and detail level for experience, education, and projects.
cv.coverSectionHint: Short letters for different roles. Enable the section in visibility to print them.
cv.coverEmptyHint: Add short letters (under ~200 words often works) tailored to different openings.
cv.printLayoutHint: For a 1-page target, typography scales with the header prioritized; hide sections or shorten long blocks so it still reads well.

pricing.warningBody: The amounts shown are indicative and not yet finalized commercially. Pricing and terms may change before billing goes live.
pricing.warningPatreon: We’re also considering options like a lifetime price and/or Patreon for folks who want to support development. Nothing is decided yet—we’ll shape it over time with feedback.
pricing.heroTitle: Straightforward plans for your skills and portfolio.
pricing.heroSubtitle: Limited invites today. Pricing for people who want consistency—not another empty board.
pricing.faq1Q: Can I use SkillAtlas without paying today?
pricing.faq1A: During invite-only early access, the Starter plan covers the core product. Paid plans will go live when billing opens.
pricing.faq2A: Priority support, billing for multiple seats under one account, and alignment for teams. Final details are agreed at purchase.
pricing.faq3A: We are not designing for forced lock-ins. Annual billing reflects a commitment discount; you can adjust plans as terms evolve.
pricing.ctaSubtitle: Request access and we will reply as soon as there is capacity.
pricing.starterDesc: For early adoption: skill map and a coherent portfolio.
pricing.proDesc: Depth and polish when you publish seriously.
pricing.teamDesc: When several people share documentation standards.
pricing.notePaidYear: Monthly equivalent · annual billing · save vs monthly
pricing.lifetimeSubtitle: Indicative prices for early adopters; real billing will go live with the payment gateway.
pricing.lifetimeProDesc: Same as Pro, no renewals while the service is offered.
pricing.lifetimeProF2: No recurring fees while SkillAtlas offers the plan
pricing.lifetimeTeamDesc: For small teams who want to lock cost upfront.

landing.title: SkillAtlas
landing.subtitle: Manage your technical knowledge: technologies, concepts, progress and projects that become your portfolio.
landing.step1Body: Create categories like SQL, PySpark or DAX.
landing.step3Body: Link concepts and publish your portfolio.
landing.heroSubtitle: Organize stack and concepts, document projects with evidence, and share your portfolio via readable slug or revocable link. Private CV with preview and print; OG previews when you share.
landing.noteEvidence: So your portfolio doesn’t depend on “I think I remember…”, but on evidence and a clear story (role + outcome).
landing.noteInvite: Invite-only while we iterate calmly toward a v1 that earns the name.
landing.previewProjectBody: A clear story with role and outcome—impact without extra context.
landing.previewPortfolioBody: Cards with story and stack, tech filter, accessible evidence, and share-friendly previews.
landing.devStrip: Product evolution and work in progress:
landing.flowSubtitle: A visual thread: paste URLs, tell the story, ship.
landing.flowCard1Body: Paste URLs and we detect embed vs link in seconds.
landing.flowCard2Body: Role + outcome so the project reads without extra context.
landing.flowCard3Body: Turn sharing on: readable URL or revocable token—you choose when and how to show it.
landing.faqIntro: Straight answers if you are browsing without a session. For anything specific, use Contact below.
landing.faq1A: To iterate without friction and protect quality while the product grows deliberately.
landing.faq2A: This landing with visual demos, guide pricing, and the contact page without signing in. If someone shares their portfolio, that public URL is visible too. Everything else needs an invite and a session.
landing.faq3A: Use “Request access” and tell us your use case. We reply when there’s room.
landing.faq4A: We publish it in the versioned backlog. Among other themes: more templates, portfolio PDFs, duplicate project, and billing when it’s time.
landing.faq5A: With a session, the core lives in Supabase (PostgreSQL) with per-account RLS. Toolkit tools may stay in your browser until you turn on cloud storage where available.
landing.faq6Q: Do I have to publish a portfolio or CV?
landing.faq6A: No. You can keep everything private and turn on public links only when you want, from Settings.
landing.faq7Q: What are the prices on the site?
landing.faq7A: A guide for teams and personal use. Real charges will ship with clear terms when billing goes live.
landing.faq8A: Today SkillAtlas is a modern web app in the browser. We prioritise desktop flow; mobile improves incrementally.
landing.roadmapTitle: Shipped, in progress, and what’s next
landing.roadmapSubtitle: We update this as things land; several features are already live—the rest is still cooking.
landing.rmPricingBody: A public page with guide pricing and comparison; real charges arrive with clear terms.
landing.rmShareBody: Readable slug (/portfolio/…), revocable token link (/p/…), toggle from Settings, safe public read via RPC.
landing.rmCvBody: Private editor with chosen projects, preview, print-friendly output, optional public link evolving.
landing.rmExportBody: Portfolio PDFs, richer previews per project, static export when it fits.
landing.rmDailyBody: Already: recent activity on /app (synced when signed in), command palette (Ctrl+K), shortcuts panel and checklist. Next: templates, more palette actions.
landing.rmBillingBody: Gateway, subscriptions, plan limits when demand justifies it—still aiming for humane pricing.
landing.todayIntro: With an invite you get the full core: organize knowledge and present it with taste.
landing.todayLi1: Create and organize technologies, concepts, and projects in one place, synced when you are signed in.
landing.todayLi2: Evidence as link or embed, ordered, with hints from the URL.
landing.todayLi3: Project story: role + outcome to explain impact, not just tasks.
landing.todayLi4: Public portfolio by slug or token, profile and sharing from Settings; landing tool demos, pricing, and access requests with an admin queue.
landing.todayLi5: Dark mode, English and Spanish, smooth navigation, sidebar-style Settings, guided tour, and small quality-of-life improvements.
landing.chartsPreviewBody: On /app you’ll see real data. Here’s a fictional 2025 sample with cards similar to the dashboard (stack and activity).
landing.chartsPreviewTrendTitle: Work rhythm in 2025 (sample, not real data)
landing.chartsPreviewStackHint: Fictional sample; /app ranks from your projects.
landing.chartsPreviewActivityIntro: Static sample; with a session the list syncs like /app.
landing.toolsShowcaseTitle: A taste of the in-app tools
landing.toolsShowcaseIntro: These are static demos with sample data so you can feel the UI. Links to real tools require a session—if you are not signed in, we send you to /login and remember where to return.
landing.toolsShowcaseEtIntro: Subscriptions, period expenses, income, balance, charts, and a natural-year projection—mirroring what you see in the app.
landing.toolsShowcaseEtSubsHint: Tap a card to edit (in the real tool).
landing.toolsShowcaseEtKpiBalHint: Income − expenses in the current month
landing.toolsShowcaseEtYearIncHint: Monthly sum in the current year
landing.toolsShowcaseEtYearNetHint: Income − outflows (same rules as the chart)
landing.toolsShowcaseHabitsIntro: Month grid, quick add, and tactile day cells—stored on-device in the real tool.
landing.toolsShowcaseHabitsTip: Tip: click a cell to toggle (in the app).
landing.toolsShowcasePomoIntro: Focus timer with local daily counter—same chrome you get in-session.
landing.toolsShowcaseMoreSubtitle: Handy day-to-day utilities; links go into the app (session required).
landing.toolsShowcaseMoreConvertDesc: Images and files in the browser.
landing.toolsShowcaseMoreReadmeDesc: Sanitized Markdown—paste or load from URL.
landing.toolsShowcaseMoreDiffDesc: Compare two texts line by line.
landing.toolsShowcaseMoreTalkDesc: Countdown with cues to rehearse talks.
landing.facetsIntro: Drag the track, swipe, or use the middle dots: projects, stack, portfolio, CV, tools, and study. Auto-advances every few seconds; the end dots are not buttons.

projects.title: Projects
projects.body: Stories with role and outcome, linked or embedded evidence, and a clear stack.

tech.title: Technologies
tech.body: Your live map: what you’ve mastered and what’s next—without losing the thread.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.body: Readable slug or revocable link; clean cards when you want to show real work.

cv.title: CV
cv.body: Private by default, preview and print; optional public link.

tools.title: Tools
tools.body: Expenses, habits, Pomodoro, and browser utilities: demos on the landing and the full kit at /tools with a session.

study.title: Study
study.body: Sources and review in one flow; the /study workspace keeps growing.

landing.ctaBottomBody: Request access with your use case, skim guide pricing, or write us if you want to talk before signing in.

study.title: Study workspace
study.intro: Suggested flow: goal → link technologies/project in SkillAtlas → (optional) syllabus → PDFs/links/code sources → documentation-style notes, search, and dossiers. For SQL-like stacks, import concepts under Technologies and link them here; in notes use Markdown and fenced code (e.g. sql) for documentation-style blocks.
study.introWorkSurface: Work with sources, search, dossiers, and notes here. Study name, technologies, and syllabus live under “Study context” (modal).
study.newSpaceModalIntro: Each study has its own sources, notes, and syllabus. You can refine context later in “Study context”.
study.newSpaceError: Could not create the study (check Supabase or saas-027 migration).
study.multiStudyHint: Multiple studies per account, each with its own sources, notes, and syllabus. Apply saas-027 on Supabase if you have not yet.
study.setupDialogIntro: Set the name, link technologies, and optionally your syllabus. Then close and use the main view for materials and work.
study.setupDoneToast: Context saved. Continue with sources and notes.
study.studyNameHint: Exam, certification, or topic. Shown as your active study and on the dashboard.
study.linkedConceptsIntro: With linked technologies (e.g. SQL), concepts from your catalog appear here; links open SkillAtlas tech detail to go deeper or import more.
study.linkedConceptsNeedTech: Link at least one technology above to see its concepts here.
study.linkedConceptsEmpty: No concepts on those technologies yet. Create them or use “Load suggested catalog” on Technologies.
study.linkedConceptsTruncated: Showing part of the list; open the technology to see every concept.
study.userNotesHintDoc: Markdown, fenced code (triple backtick + sql, dax, …) and images via ![alt](url). Documentation / Notion style.
study.needSupabase: Sign in to upload files to your workspace.
study.uploadAssociationHint: Tip: if you upload a PDF after filling title/URL, it will be associated to this entry.
study.sourcesHint: Check sources you want in context for the next step (AI).
study.sourcesToolbarHint: «Add folder» and «Concepts» sit next to the folder list. Linked SkillAtlas technologies group by tech and concept subfolders. Drag the gripper to move a source. Use «+ Add» for code, links, or notes.
study.emptySourcesInFolders: No sources yet. Use «+ Add» or «Upload» above.
study.folderModalIntro: Custom folders are saved with your study space.
study.conceptsModalIntro: Concepts live under each technology. See how many you have and open the tech detail to create or import the suggested catalog.
study.conceptsModalCreateHint: Create concepts or import the catalog from the technology page.
study.confirmDeleteCustomFolder: Delete this folder? Sources in it will move to General.
study.searchNeedSession: Sign in to search in your sources.
study.chatPlaceholder: Ask about your sources (context uses checked sources; answers should cite [[1]], [[2]]…).
study.chatInputPlaceholder: e.g. “Difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in my syllabus”…
study.chatDisabledHint: Chat with sources is not available in this environment.
study.chatDisabledEnv: Chat is not enabled on the server.
study.chatHint: Tap a [[1]] citation to preview the quote and jump to the source.
study.chatDisabledToast: Chat is disabled on the server.
study.chatFallbackCitations: Retrieved excerpts, but the assistant did not respond. Please try again later.
study.chatEmpty: Ask something about your sources; answers include citations like [[1]], [[2]]…
study.dossierInputPlaceholder: Type a question or topic… (e.g. “WHERE vs HAVING”)
study.dossierNeedContext: Select at least one source in context.
study.goalHint: Optional label for your syllabus or exam. Saved to your account and shown on the dashboard.
study.goalSaveErrorToast: Could not save the goal. Check your connection and try again.
study.goalNeedSessionToast: Sign in to save your goal to your account.
study.dossiersHint: Saved evidence packs; synced across devices when signed in.
study.userNotesHint: Synced to your account when you are signed in. Code-style notes can use a language label for clarity.
study.userNotesNeedSql: Saved notes are not available right now. Please try again later.
study.studioHint: Assisted generation: coming in next iterations.
study.emptySources: No sources yet. Add a link or a note.
study.sourceCodeHint: Included in search and chat together with your other sources.
study.sourceCodeComposeHint: Syntax highlighting, line numbers, and find (Ctrl+F or Cmd+F).
study.codeWorkbenchIntro: Highlighting, gutters, and search. Saving updates the source and reindexes it for search and dossier.
study.workbenchHint: IDE-style panel: open a code file from Sources.
study.workDockEmpty: No code file open. In Sources, click a “Code” source title to open it here.
study.linkSkillAtlasIntro: Link technologies from your catalog to this workspace. The project is optional. You add new libraries yourself under Technologies — we do not try to mirror all of npm or PyPI.
study.linkTechCatalogEmpty: No technologies in your catalog yet. Use «Add technology», npm/PyPI or GitHub below, or create them under Technologies.
study.linkSaveError: Could not save SkillAtlas links. Please try again.
study.linkProjectOptionalHint: Only if you want this workspace tied to one project.
study.curriculumIntro: Break the syllabus into blocks (e.g. SQL, Python) and track each topic. Syncs with your account.
study.curriculumEmpty: Add a block (e.g. SQL, Python) and split it into topics.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Projects and technologies first: evidence, portfolio, and CV when you want to polish details.
dashboard.goProjectsBody: Link concepts and publish your portfolio.
dashboard.summaryIntro: Quick links to your technologies and projects (lists refresh when you are signed in).
dashboard.mockHydrateHint: Sign in to load your A–Z lists here, or open Technologies and Projects from the header.
dashboard.signedOutListHint: Sign in to load your project and technology lists.

kpi.allTechsHaveConcepts: Every technology has at least one concept.

recent.sectionIntro: The last items you opened. With a session, the list syncs across browsers via your account.
recent.emptyProjects: No recent projects yet. Open one from Projects.
recent.emptyTech: No recent technologies yet. Open one from Technologies.

study.title: Your prep workspace
study.intro: Sources, indexed excerpts, and notes tied to your account (and dossiers when you save them).
study.partialHint: Some study stats could not be loaded. Please try again later.

charts.sectionIntro: Projects and technologies first; concepts show in stacked bars and coverage when you are signed in.
charts.needSession: Sign in to see charts with your data.
charts.projectsHint: Project ↔ technology links in your data.
charts.gaugeHint: Share of concepts in the Mastered state.
charts.treemapHint: Each block is a technology; size reflects concept count.
charts.treemapProjectsHint: Size reflects project ↔ technology links (more projects, more area).
charts.coverageHint: Concepts linked to projects versus concepts only under technologies.
charts.noTechLink: Some concepts are not linked to a known technology; check your data.

technologies.title: Technologies
technologies.catalogSectionIntro: Add a name, pick a type, and set the URL identifier if you want a stable link.
technologies.multiModeTip: In multi mode you can create several technologies in one save.
technologies.kindHint: Libraries and packages can include child technologies you define.
technologies.parentTechHint: Group this library or package under a base tech (e.g. Python, React). Requires SQL migration saas-028 on Supabase.
technologies.slugUrlIdHint: Used in links to this technology. Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens.
technologies.importSectionTitle: Import from npm, PyPI or GitHub
technologies.importSectionIntro: Look up a package to pre-fill details, or import a repository from GitHub.
technologies.registryHint: Try npm/react, pypi/pandas, or a package URL from npmjs.com / pypi.org.
technologies.registryMultiDisabled: Exit multi mode to look up npm/PyPI.
technologies.registryNeedQuery: Enter a package id (npm/…, pypi/…) or paste the registry URL.
technologies.registryNeedSession: Sign in to query the registry.
technologies.slugConflictUnique: This URL identifier conflicts with another record. Try a different slug or name. If it keeps happening, contact support.

projects.evidenceThumbUrlPlaceholder: Empty → automatic YouTube or Open Graph
projects.evidenceThumbUploadHint: Upload replaces the URL. Max 8 MB; compressed in the browser.
projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Projects connected to technologies and concepts.
projects.emptyTitle: You don't have any projects yet.
projects.emptyBody: Create your first project from here.
projects.techRemoveButtonTitle: Remove from project or delete technology
projects.statusWorkflowHint: CRM-style flow (Bitrix-like): draft → in progress → published on your public portfolio, or archived. Each project has its own status; evidence items can be toggled separately when you edit them.
projects.editOutcomePlaceholder: What was achieved or what the project demonstrates
projects.editDateOrderError: End date must be on or after start date.
projects.techRemoveUnlinkHint: The technology stays in your catalog and in other projects.
projects.techRemoveGlobalHint: Deletes the technology, its concepts, and links across all projects.
projects.duplicateConfirmBody: Creates a copy with the same stack, linked concepts, and evidence. Cover image is not copied.
projects.duplicateNeedSession: Sign in to duplicate a project.
projects.duplicateSlugError: Could not reserve a unique slug.
projects.duplicateRelationsError: Project created, but some links may be incomplete.
projects.studyBannerBody: Your study workspace is linked to this project ({{count}} sources).

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.noEmbedsMock: No evidence to show in this preview.
portfolio.techFilterHint: Pick one or more technologies, then «Apply».
portfolio.techFilterNoMatch: No projects match this filter. Try other technologies or «All».

public.noEvidenceOwner: No evidence yet. Add some from the project detail page.

technologyDetail.emptyConceptsTitle: You don't have concepts in this technology yet.
technologyDetail.emptyConceptsBody: Concepts are created from this page.
technologyDetail.emptyProjectsTitle: There are no projects using {{name}} yet.
technologyDetail.emptyProjectsBody: Go to Projects to start linking concepts.
technologyDetail.studyBannerBody: This technology is linked to your Study workspace (SkillAtlas).

dashboard.layoutToolbarLabel: Settings toolbar: session, theme, columns and account actions

shell.toolbarLabel: Settings toolbar: session, theme, sidebar and account actions

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Personalize the interface, your public profile and shortcuts. Syncs when you are signed in.

shortcuts.intro: Shortcuts work across the app (except while typing in an input).
shortcuts.paletteNavigate: In the palette: move and open
shortcuts.slashHint: Slash (/) opens the same palette when focus is not in a text field.
shortcuts.cvLinkPlaceholder: CV: in link fields, Right Arrow to accept the suggested URL

portfolio.applyPublicLinksHint: The checkbox and URL identifier are not saved until you click Apply. “Save profile” in another section still saves name, bio, layout and presentation.
portfolio.publicLinksSaveError: Could not save visibility or slug.
portfolio.applyNeedsSession: Sign in to apply these changes.
portfolio.saveScopeHint: “Save profile” (public profile) saves name, bio and stack; public links use their own Apply button in that section.
portfolio.publicDisplayMigrationShort: These options also affect visitors: default layout, how many evidence items show per project, and the optional hero button.
portfolio.presentationMigration: Theme, accent, density, and featured project order apply to your public portfolio when saved.
portfolio.hint: Text shown at the top of your public portfolio.
portfolio.publicUrlHint: Configure public portfolio visibility, your /portfolio/… address, optional token link, and public CV link. Sign in required.
portfolio.publicLinksPortfolioSectionIntro: Visibility, URL slug, and revocable /p/… link for your portfolio page.
portfolio.publicLinksCvSectionIntro: Separate revocable link for your résumé (managed here alongside portfolio settings).
portfolio.tokenHint: Revocable link (more private than the slug). Requires public visibility to be enabled.
portfolio.tokenWarn: When you regenerate, the previous link stops working.
portfolio.slugInvalid: To publish you need a valid identifier: 2–32 characters, lowercase letters, numbers or hyphens (no reserved words).
portfolio.slugTaken: That identifier is already taken. Try another.
portfolio.copyNeedsValidSlug: Choose a valid identifier before copying the link.
portfolio.copyFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard.
portfolio.savedProfileSlugNeedsMigration: Profile saved. Your public portfolio address could not be updated—try again later.
portfolio.publicDisplayHint: Set the default layout, how many evidence items to show per project, and an optional button under your bio. Visitors can still override layout and count on their device.
portfolio.publicDisplayMigration: Extra layout options for your public portfolio.
portfolio.savedProfileDisplayNeedsMigration: Profile saved. Some public layout settings could not be synced—try again later.
portfolio.heroCtaNeedsUrl: If you set a button label, add a valid https URL too.
portfolio.heroCtaUrlInvalid: The button URL must be http or https.
portfolio.presentationHint: Affects visitors and the preview on /portfolio. Other global preferences are in other Settings sections.
portfolio.featuredHintEmpty: Create projects to see their slugs here.
portfolio.featuredSlugsFiltered: Some lines were skipped because they do not match any project slug.
portfolio.savedProfile014Migration: Profile saved. Some theme or presentation options could not be saved—try again later.

qa.hint: Tools to test, seed data, and report issues.
qa.needSession: Sign in to create test data.
qa.noSupabase: Cloud features are not available in this environment.

helpStack.title: Help stack
helpStack.subtitle: Productivity and AI tools you use beyond the concept catalog.

prefs.localFirstHint: Changes apply instantly across the app.
prefs.fontGoogleHint: Fonts in these groups load from Google Fonts when selected.
prefs.uiFontScaleHint: Affects the whole UI; if layout breaks, use Medium.
prefs.motionHint: “Reduced” aligns better with system reduced motion.
prefs.showHeaderIconsHint: CV, technologies, projects, portfolio in the top bar.
prefs.showLangSelectorHint: Shows region flag; UI stays Spanish.
prefs.headerLangPopoverHint: How to open the language menu.
prefs.headerUserMenuPopoverHint: How to open the account menu.
prefs.headerHomePopoverHint: How to open “Choose destination” (/app vs landing).
prefs.fabCvTipsHint: Shows the tab with application and PDF export tips.

auth.magicToggle: Email magic link (counts toward hourly email cap)
auth.magicHelp: Useful if you prefer no password; free tier limits outbound emails per hour.
auth.noSessionBanner: Sign in to sync with the cloud.

guidedTour.restartedToast: Tour restarted. Follow the steps in the floating panel.
guidedTour.continueLaterToast: You can resume anytime from the dashboard: “Guided tour”.

onboardingV2.youDecideSaveTech: Type the name and click Save when you’re ready.
onboardingV2.youDecideSaveProject: Review the example and click Save project when you’re ready.
onboardingV2.youDecideSaveSettings: When it looks good, click Save profile.
onboardingV2.seedLoaded: Catalog loaded. Now generate a preview or import quickly.
onboardingV2.noSeedHere: This technology has no suggested catalog.
onboardingV2.step1Body: Use the search to create a technology (for example: Tableau, Python, dbt…).
onboardingV2.step2Body: On a technology detail page, you can load a suggested catalog and import concepts quickly.
onboardingV2.step3Body: Fill in title, description, and (optional) role and impact for context.
onboardingV2.step4Body: Paste a Tableau Public link and add it as an iframe to see how it renders in the portfolio.
onboardingV2.step5Body: Fill in your name and bio, adjust portfolio/CV privacy, and add your help stack.
onboardingV2.step6Body: See how your portfolio looks and keep adding more projects.
onboardingV2.step7Body: Open Preview to see your full CV, then print/save as PDF.
